Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF EVA SMALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Conservatorship [Due To Death - Accounting Required]
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine why the final/supplemental account has not yet been filed by the conservator, when the conservatee died on 8/5/22, and an administrator was appointed for the conservatee's probate estate on 11/9/22. Conservator's attorneys also represent the administrator of the conservatee's estate, which creates a potential conflict, as the conservator and his attorney will likely be creditors of the estate if they seek and are awarded fees for services in this proceeding. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 3/24/23 shows that notice of the First Account and Report was served. An AMENDED First Account and Report was filed. Notice must clearly indicate that it is of the AMENDED petition. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] (4) The conservatorship or trust case number must be noted in parentheses under any pleading caption in the related matter. [LRSF 14.47 B.] The amended pleading has not yet been fully reviewed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHRERINE ANNE PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/24/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An un-endorsed filed copy of a "Supplement" was received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 C.] It appears that the "Supplement" was filed on 3/22/23. If the content of the unfiled paper is the same as the one that was actually filed, an amendment should have been filed, which requires notice. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53(a).] (2) A fiduciary is required to sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (3) If an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (5) Distribution cannot be made to an "estate". Has Warren James Phillips Jr. been appointed by a court as the personal representative of the estate of Warren James Phillips? A certified copy of his Letters must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 G.] (6) What happened to the 2008 Ford Escape Hybrid appraised at $3,500? (7) The property on hand shown on the Summary of Account does not appear to be quite correct; it should be $266,095.00. (8) Filing fees shown on the disbursements schedule should show WHAT was filed or must be itemized. (9) This Court rarely permits more than $45/hour for non-professional fiduciaries, especially when the time spent did not require any special skills, no matter how daunting the task is of sorting through a decedent's many possessions. (10) The administrator's time entries show many boxes of books -- and he is requesting compensation for spending many hours boxing them -- but no books were inventoried. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required for the compensation requested for extraordinary services. In addition to the issue of the administrator's hourly rate, attorney time spent to increase bond is not extraordinary.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERIC P. JENSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Proposed Order must be submitted before the hearing. The order to mirror the changes brought by the supplemental declaration filed 8/19/2022. 2. Acceptance of trusteeship from the current trustee (Crystal Elledge) of the Eric Peter Jensen Trust that is the sole devisee under the terms of the trust. PC 10954(b)(4). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] The papers were reviewed online as ONE-TIME COURTESY in hopes of being able to pregrant the petition. Item 8 of the petition, however, is missing the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. [LRSF 14.18 C.3 and D.] Deadline is 5/1/23 for providing a courtesy copy to the assigned Examiner of the petitioner's verified supplement to her petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
